openingQuery: Can you connect me with a real Pellier stylist? I want a person to help me pick what to wear to my brother's wedding – not product cards.

chat.content: Can you connect me with a real Pellier stylist? I want a person to help me pick what to wear to my brother's wedding – not product cards.

I've routed your note to a Pellier stylist. They'll reach out within one business day with personal styling for the wedding – silhouette, palette, and how the moment feels – beyond what our catalog tools can answer alone. Keep browsing in the meantime; we'll pick up where you left off.

toolCalls.description: Hand off to a human stylist with a one-sentence reason and persona context
toolCalls.resultSummary: Stylist handoff queued · response window 1 business day

brief.headline: When the honest answer is 'a real person'

sections.title: Why escalate, not search
paragraphs: Marco asked for a stylist by name – to dress him for his brother's wedding. Pellier's catalog tools (find_pieces, style_match, side_by_side) are designed for piece-level questions: silhouette, fabric, fit-as-described. They cannot judge how a body looks in motion at an event, or read the shopper's relationship to the day.

The Style Advisor recognizes the gap and calls escalate_to_stylist with a one-sentence reason. The orchestrator's stylist-handoff branch routes the explicit request to search instead of refusing as 'outside shopping.'

sections.title: Honest fallback, not lazy fallback
paragraphs: Every specialist has escalate_to_stylist in its toolset. Every system prompt instructs the agent to ALWAYS try catalog tools first; escalate_to_stylist is the honest exit when the ask genuinely sits outside what the tools can answer. The orchestrator routes by topic – search for wardrobe, recommendation for sentiment, support for exception returns – and the inner specialist makes the final call.
The handoff payload includes a stylist contact, a 1-business-day response window, and next steps. The shopper is not stranded; they are routed.
